meta.title: EU Legal Pathways — SafePassage
meta.description: All legal routes into the European Union — asylum, blue card, study, family, humanitarian visa, and resettlement.

hero.title: Legal Pathways to Europe
hero.sub: The European Union and its member states offer multiple legal routes to live, work, study and seek protection — without risking your life at sea. Choose the route that matches your situation.

cards.name: EU Asylum
cards.desc: Every EU member state is bound by the 1951 Refugee Convention. You can claim asylum in any EU country where you are present. The process is free.

cards.name: EU Blue Card
cards.desc: The EU Blue Card is the main work visa for skilled professionals. It offers high salary rights, family reunification, and a path to permanent residence.

cards.name: Study in Europe
cards.desc: Many EU universities charge little or no tuition — including in Germany, Norway, and Finland. Scholarships from DAAD and Erasmus+ are available.

cards.name: Family Reunification
cards.desc: If a close family member has legal status in an EU country, you can apply to join them through EU Family Reunification Directive rights.

cards.name: EU Resettlement
cards.desc: The EU and member states run resettlement programmes working with UNHCR to bring vulnerable refugees to Europe safely and legally.

cards.name: Humanitarian Visa
cards.desc: Some EU countries issue humanitarian visas at embassies, allowing legal entry and an asylum application on arrival — without dangerous crossings.
